notes: Thin roster on purpose: two roles, both directly tied to first-vintage release work. Sub-agent secondaries are adjacent to my decommissioned-textile-mill secondary through different paths: small-press poetry editing shares the preservation-versus-smoothing tension of rehab work, and volunteer-fire operations shares the coordinate-independent-parties-under-time-pressure pattern of running a rehab site.

entries.primary_description: Writes owned-channel copy: storefront product descriptions, mailing-list emails, wine-club onboarding sequences, and in-bottle producer notes. Works to 80-to-300 word deliverables most of the time. Stays inside TTB-compliant claims and matches the technical sheet.
entries.suggested_adjacency: Pull from a field where specificity and restraint are the craft: small-press editing, letter-writing traditions, museum-label writing, short-form liner-note traditions. NOT journalism and NOT corporate marketing writing.
entries.notes: Selected Candidate B (small-press poetry editor). Candidate A's secondary was museum exhibition-label writing, which was adjacent but the voice read more institutional than I wanted. Candidate C was a wire-service journalist, rejected for over-valuing speed over craft on short-form work.

entries.primary_description: Positioning, narrative architecture, visual-identity briefs, naming, and the strategic choices about what the brand says and does not say. Produces briefs that copywriters and designers execute against. Does not produce finished assets.
entries.suggested_adjacency: Pull from a field where time-pressured decision-making and commitment under uncertainty are load-bearing skills: emergency services, live theater direction, small-boat racing, volunteer-led organizations with high stakes. This role is about defending brand boundaries under temptation, which rhymes with incident command.
entries.notes: Selected Candidate A on first slate; the incident-command framing fit the role's decision-under-pressure profile better than the two alternatives (small-boat racing, community-theater direction).

candidates.label: Candidate B
candidates.declared_secondary: small-boat racing, specifically skiff-class handicap fleets
